key_drivers: The U.S. dollar weakened to 98.709 on the day, while the Fed remains overwhelmingly priced for a neutral hold into the April 30 meeting.
Volatility remains defensive rather than supportive for broad equities: VIX is 25.77 and rising, while MOVE rose to 83.1452 even as SOX advanced 1.11%.

The macro growth picture is mixed: Atlanta Fed GDPNow was 1.3% on April 7, China’s official manufacturing PMI rebounded to 50.4 in March, and euro-area growth projections were revised lower by the ECB.

Cross-asset and FX divergence remains important: the RBA hiked to 4.10%, the ECB delivered a non-dovish hold, and the BoJ kept the overnight call rate around 0.75% while risk-off conditions continue to support the yen.
